data.title: Keystone Global Financial files for IPO of 1.25M shares
data.description:
Keystone Global Financial Group has filed a preliminary prospectus for an initial public offering of 1.25M ordinary shares, representing 10% of the total issued and outstanding shares.

The Cayman Islands holding company provides financial and strategic advisory services to the infrastructure and construction industries.

The prospectus filed on Form F-1 with the Securities and Exchange Commission is not an offer to sell.

The IPO is expected to be priced between $4.00 and $6.00 per ordinary share, and the company plans to reserve KCG as its ticker symbol on the Nasdaq Capital Market.

data.title: Zenas BioPharma rises after Wedbush raises price target
data.description:
Shares of Zenas BioPharma (NASDAQ:ZBIO) rose on Thursday after Wedbush raised its price target, pointing to the company’s expanding late-stage pipeline and successful asset in-licensing strategy.

The price target for the company was raised to $40 from $35.

On Wednesday, the company in-licensed multiple assets from InnoCare Pharma, including orelabrutinib, a BTK inhibitor currently in phase 3 development for progressive forms of multiple sclerosis.

Wedbush analysts said that the addition of these assets strengthen Zenas’s growth outlook and adds meaningful diversification to its portfolio.

Wedbush also highlighted near-term clinical catalysts that could further enhance ZBIO’s valuation, including the anticipated Phase 3 INDIGO trial results for obexelimab in IgG4-related disease by year-end 2025 and Phase 2 MoonStone trial results in MS expected in early fourth-quarter 2025. 

Wedbus reiterated Outperform rating. The shares of the company are up about 3% today.

data.title: Scholar Rock wins Buy at BofA on blockbuster potential for lead drug
data.description:
Scholar Rock (NASDAQ:SRRK) traded higher on Thursday after Bank of America initiated its coverage with a Buy recommendation, citing a blockbuster sales potential for the biotech’s lead asset, apitegromab, targeted at a rare neuromuscular disease called spinal muscular atrophy (SMA).

Analyst Tazeen Ahmad’s bullish view came despite the U.S. FDA’s recent decision to decline marketing authorization for apitegromab in SMA due to issues at a third-party fill-finish facility.

Ahmad noted that the setback marked a delay rather than a risk to the drug’s approval, and the agency is expected to greenlight it following a potential resubmission of the market application once Scholar Rock's (NASDAQ:SRRK) manufacturing partner resolves the regulatory concerns.

Noting that all three SMA drugs have each generated more than $1B in sales last year, the analyst projected $2B in risk-adjusted peak sales for apitegromab in 2037, making up $44 of her $53 per share target on Scholar Rock (NASDAQ:SRRK).

“While approved SMA therapies (Spinraza, Evrysdi, Zolgensma) reduce progressive motor neuron loss, significant unmet need remains, as these are not curative and disease progression persists,” Ahmad argued.

The analyst added that apitegromab is the first SMA therapy to show a positive effect on the disease, and it can address a key symptom of the disease, as it is designed to inhibit myostatin protein, which breaks down muscle.

data.title: Fox Entertainment buys stake in vertical video producer Holywater
data.description:
Fox Entertainment, a division of Fox Corporation (NASDAQ:FOX) (FOXA), has invested in vertical video company Holywater to capitalize on an $8B industry as well as the popularity of TikTok, Instagram (META), and YouTube Shorts (GOOG) (GOOGL).

Vertical videos is a format where the height of the video is greater than the width and meant to be viewed vertically on a smartphone. The videos are commonly viewed on social media platforms.

With over 55M users, Holywater operates platforms such as My Drama, My Passion, and My Muse, and airs popular dramas like The Shy Beauty and the Billionaire Beast, and Pregnant Cinderella, according to Deadline.

As part of Fox’s (NASDAQ:FOX) (FOXA) investment, the entertainment studio will create and produce more than 200 vertical videos for My Drama over the next two years.

“Innovation in digital storytelling is shaping the future of entertainment and investing in vertical video positions Fox Entertainment to achieve sustained, long-term growth as we build a modern studio for the future,” said Rob Wade, CEO of Fox Entertainment.

The amount of Fox’s (NASDAQ:FOX) (FOXA) investment was not disclosed.

The development did little to stem the pressure on Fox (FOX) (FOXA) shares, which continue to drift lower for the seventh straight day.

data.title: Gold surges on de-dollarization, debasement, and diversification – analyst
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and other metals are experiencing significant upward momentum driven by three key factors: de-dollarization, debasement concerns, and investor diversification, according to Jeff Currie, chief strategy officer of energy pathways at Carlyle.

In an interview with CNBC, Currie noted that central banks now hold 27% of their reserves in gold, exceeding their holdings in U.S. Treasury securities, as part of a de-dollarization trend that has been ongoing for more than five years.

“We can add in the debasement trade, which really started to gain steam with the government shutdown, that we saw the ETF have 110 tons of inflows recently around the debasement,” he said.

The third factor driving metals is diversification, as investors seek alternatives to tight credit spreads in fixed income and stretched equity markets.

For individual investors looking to capitalize on the metals rally, Currie recommends ETFs (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR) as an excellent investment vehicle, particularly for gold.

“The ETF is an excellent way to play it because it’s one of the few ETFs in commodities where you actually own the physical bars,” he said.

Copper (HG1:COM) presents what Currie describes as a “perfect storm” of conditions, with prices up nearly 25% year-to-date. The industrial metal faces long-term underinvestment challenges while experiencing unexpected demand surges from AI data centers and defense-related needs, all atop the ongoing electrification trend. Supply issues at three major mines in Indonesia, Chile, and the Democratic Republic of Congo have further tightened the market, creating a deficit situation.

Despite the broader commodity momentum, crude oil (CL1:COM) has remained under pressure due to concerns about a supply glut. However, Currie emphasized that this anticipated oversupply has not significantly impacted prices, with Brent Crude (CO1:COM) maintaining levels around $65-66 per barrel.

“If it was going to impact prices, it would have already impacted prices,” he noted, adding that market conditions suggest refineries will likely process rather than store available oil.

Below are commodities ETFs:

  • Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST).
  • Silver and Silver Mining ETFs: (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), (PSLV), (SIL), (SILJ), and (SLVP).
  • Copper and Copper Mining ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), and (OTC:JJCTF).
  • Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).
  • Natural Gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), and (UNL)

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: META, AMD, TSLA, DLTR
data.description:
Recent analyst actions include changes in rating for Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Meta (NASDAQ:META), highlighting significant shifts in the landscape driven by valuation pressures and company-specific performance trends. While Tesla faces challenges due to declining fundamentals and overvaluation, Meta is experiencing growth driven by increased ad impressions and its investment in AI. Meanwhile, Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD) has seen an optimistic upgrade, reflecting its strategic positioning within the tech sector. These mixed ratings underline the varying impacts of market dynamics across different sectors.

Upgrades

  • Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META): Upgrade Sell to Buy by Gary Alexander. The significant increase in ad impressions has fueled exceptional revenue growth, supporting a stronger outlook for Meta.

    “The healthy performance of Meta’s ad platform and its still-rich margin profile are helping to fund the company’s … ambitions in AI.”

  • Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD): Upgrade Hold to Strong Buy by Weebler Finance. A pivotal partnership with OpenAI enhances AMD’s strategic positioning in the AI and data center markets.

    “AMD becoming a critical hardware partner to one of the most important AI distributors in the world … delivers massive validation to the company’s GPU capabilities.”

Downgrades

  • Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA): Downgrade Hold to Sell by Florian Muller. The analyst cites a disconnect between Tesla’s soaring stock valuation and its declining fundamentals, raising concerns over sustainability.

    “Since June, Tesla stock has surged another 38%… market-based Enterprise Value increased… while TTM free cash flow decreased.”

  • Dollar Tree, Inc. (NASDAQ:DLTR): Downgrade to Hold by Redfox Capital Ideas. Concerns over the company’s pricing strategies and potential impacts on profit margins have led to a more cautious outlook.

    “Slower comps, rising tariffs, heavier markdowns, and leadership transitions all add layers of execution risk. Until DLTR proves that demand remains resilient through the upcoming seasonal period and pricing elasticity holds, I believe the stock will remain range-bound.”

data.title: U.S. stocks decline as Powell passes on policy outlook commentary, tech struggles
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes struggled to keep up momentum on Thursday, after the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) hit new intraday highs earlier, as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell did not comment on the economic outlook or monetary policy at a Fed event.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.4% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.5%.

Among Thursday's decliners, AppLovin (APP) shares were -5.1% after reports on Monday that the SEC was potentially probing them over data collection processes. Dell (DELL) -5.4% is also among the top decliners, as CEO said that clients appear to be constrained by their ability to access enough energy to power data center demands.

Within commodities, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) officially surpassed the $4,000 level on Wednesday, while the Silver spot silver also hit a historic $50/oz as investors piled into precious metals.

“Could this trigger a fresh wave of buying as retail investors rush to join the latest gold rush? It’s certainly possible, although such interest is likely to be in coins, jewelry and bullion. Nevertheless, every little helps. It looks as if gold may be consolidating after two months of relentless gains,” said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

“All recent attempts at a retracement have been short-lived, with every dip quickly met by aggressive buying interest. This persistent demand underscores the depth of conviction among market participants, even at such high price levels,” he added.

In other news, Fed Chair Jerome Powell gave welcome remarks before the Community Bank Conference, however, he did not mention anything about the monetary policy or the economy. In addition, Fed Governor Michael Barr said he's skeptical that the FOMC should "fully 'look through' higher inflation from import tariffs."

Also, due to the ongoing U.S. government shutdown, there are no economic data releases slated on Thursday.

“Last night saw the release of the minutes from the Federal Reserve’s September FOMC meeting… Despite this lack of clarity, the probability of two further rates cuts this year has risen, according to the CME’s FedWatch Tool, and this has helped to underpin U.S. equities. Overall, investors took the minutes in their stride, viewing them as neither overly dovish nor hawkish,” Morrison said.

Over in the bond market, yields were lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 3 basis points higher at 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.60%. A $22B, 30-Year Bond Auction tailed.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day. 

On geopolitics, Israel and Hamas have agreed to a ceasefire and hostage deal as part of the first phase of a peace plan advanced by U.S. President Donald Trump to end the war in Gaza.

Wall Street’s major averages ended higher on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 tagging new record closes, all while gold (XAUUSD:CUR) topped $4,000/oz and FOMC minutes landed.

More on markets:

data.title: SA Asks: Will the U.S. government continue taking stakes in miners?
data.description:
The Trump administration has been actively looking to invest in mining companies with domestic operations as part of a push to counter Chinese dominance in the mining of rare earths and other critical minerals, which are used to produce a wide array of technological devices.

Over the last few months, the U.S. government has taken equity stakes in MP Materials (NYSE:MP), Trilogy Metals (TMQ), and Lithium Americas (NYSE:LAC). It's also reportedly been in talks with Energy Fuels (UUU), Critical Metals (NASDAQ:CRML), and USA Rare Earth (USAR).

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Grassroots Trading and Austin Craig whether they thought the government would continue to seek out equity stakes in miners.

Will the U.S. government continue taking stakes in miners?

Grassroots Trading: Yes, absolutely, there’s a clear trend emerging, and it seems likely that the U.S. government will continue investing in mining companies, particularly those involved in critical minerals and strategic supply chains. 

Let’s walk it back to what’s been happening lately. Earlier this week, the Department of Defense bought a 10% stake in Trilogy Metals (TMQ) to support its mining projects in Alaska’s Ambler district. Since then, the company’s stock has gone up about 200%. Earlier this month, the Department of Energy took a 5% equity stake in Lithium Americas (NYSE: LAC) as part of a funding restructuring. And back in July, MP Materials (NYSE: MP), a major rare earth producer, finalized a $400 million preferred share and warrant deal, which gave the Pentagon roughly a 15% ownership position, making it the company’s largest shareholder.

I just covered Critical Metals (NASDAQ:CRML), and judging by Wednesday’s price action, it looks like the market maybe thinks the government could be getting closer to finalizing or expanding a deal there, contrary to what Bloomberg reported earlier this week. Separately, there’s growing speculation that Washington is also in talks with USA Rare Earth (USAR) about taking a stake to help strengthen U.S. supplies of rare earth materials and batteries.

That said, I don’t think this will turn into broad asset-class investing. The government’s going to limit this to two core areas: national security and supply chain vulnerability. Investors may want to pay attention to those aforementioned names and their peers. Or, if you want a broader selection, I’d go with ETFs like iShares MSCI Global Metals & Mining Producers ETF (BATS:PICK) or Global X Lithium & Battery Tech ETF (NYSEARCA:LIT) for exposure to where public policy right now is literally writing checks.

Austin Craig: I think investors need to consider a few things first when looking at companies in the sector. 

One, does this empower the Department of War? Two, is it something we need more of in order to project power? And, three, is this a mineral, element, or process dominated by China? 

Areas that I like are lithium, which is needed for drone warfare, EVs, and fixed energy for data centers. Lithium Americas (LAC) has had a run, but I am intrigued by Standard Lithium (SLI) with its approved DoE $225 million grant. 

If LAC had to agree to equity for an interest-bearing loan, it seems logical President Trump would want something in return for a $225 million grant. After all, grants are free money in a sense. 

Other sectors that interest me are helium and lithium iron phosphate, or LFP, refining for EVs. You need helium for semiconductor manufacturing, and LFP is dominated by China.

  • Top Diversified Metals and Mining Stocks

data.title: Helen of Troy outlines $1.74B–$1.78B sales target for FY26 amid leadership transition and tariff mitigation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Helen of Troy Limited (HELE) Q2 2026

Management View

  • Helen of Troy introduced Scott Uzzell as its new Chief Executive Officer, who emphasized his background in consumer-centric leadership and his goal to engineer a comeback for the company by focusing on innovation, brand revitalization, and cultural renewal. Uzzell stated, "I am clear-eyed about the challenges we are facing. I embrace the opportunity to reverse course... Our focus is to invigorate the exceptional assets we have to leverage our leadership brands and talent within our organization to win in the marketplace." He outlined four initial priorities: reenergizing brands and people, placing the consumer at the center, strengthening the portfolio for predictable growth, and improving asset efficiency while maintaining shareholder-friendly policies.
  • CFO Brian Grass welcomed Uzzell and highlighted early progress on five key priorities set last quarter, including improved go-to-market effectiveness and operational excellence. Grass noted, "While we are not at all satisfied with our results, we believe we took a step in the right direction with net sales and adjusted EPS at or above the high end of our outlook ranges." He referenced double-digit revenue growth for Hot Tools, Curlsmith, and Osprey, along with 15% DTC revenue growth year-over-year and positive free cash flow of $23 million fiscal year-to-date, despite a $34 million cash flow drag from tariffs.
  • Assistant CFO Tracy Schuerman confirmed continued focus on tariff mitigation and cost discipline, stating, "We now anticipate that we can lower our cost of goods sold subject to China tariffs to between 25% and 30% by the end of fiscal '26 as compared to our previous expectation of below 25%."

Outlook

  • The company provided a FY26 net sales outlook of $1.74 billion to $1.78 billion, which represents a decline of 8.8% to 6.7% year-over-year. Segment-wise, Home & Outdoor is expected to decline 11.8% to 9.7%, and Beauty & Wellness by 6.2% to 4%, including $130 million to $137 million from Olive & June. Full-year adjusted EPS is projected in the range of $3.75 to $4.25, a decline of 47.7% to 40.7% year-over-year.
  • For Q3, the company expects net sales between $491 million and $512 million and adjusted diluted EPS between $1.55 and $1.80. Management cited ongoing trade disruption, cautious retailer inventory management, and continued soft consumer demand, but expects mitigation from pricing actions and the Olive & June acquisition.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated net sales for the second quarter decreased 8.9%. Excluding Olive & June, organic net sales declined 16%, with about 30% of this attributed to tariff-related disruptions. Olive & June contributed $33.4 million in sales for the quarter.
  • Gross profit margin fell 140 basis points to 44.2%, with higher tariffs on COGS unfavorably impacting margin by approximately 200 basis points. Adjusted operating margin decreased 360 basis points to 6.2%. Non-GAAP adjusted EPS was $0.59, compared to $1.21 in the prior year period.
  • Inventory ended at $528.9 million, up $59 million year-over-year, impacted by the Olive & June acquisition and $32 million of tariff-related costs. Total debt stood at $893.2 million, a sequential increase of $22 million.

Q&A

  • Rupesh Parikh, Oppenheimer & Co.: Asked about the portfolio and divestiture opportunities. Uzzell responded that all brands have promise and that specific decisions will be evaluated as part of the long-range plan, but no immediate changes are planned.
  • Parikh followed up on the earnings base outlook. Grass said there are transitory impacts in FY26 that are expected to dissipate in the second half and into FY27, serving as building blocks for growth.
  • Bob Labick, CJS Securities: Inquired about revitalizing leading brands. Uzzell emphasized "obsessing the consumer and consumer insights," driving innovation, and improving decision-making speed.
  • Labick asked about leverage and capital structure. Grass said the optimal leverage target is closer to 2x, and discussions with banks have been supportive regarding potential covenant relief.
  • Susan Anderson, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about growth opportunities by segment. Uzzell and Grass highlighted upside in both Home & Outdoor and Beauty & Wellness, as well as M&A potential once the foundation is strengthened.
  • Anderson asked for color on performance within segments. Grass noted wellness as weaker due to direct import disruption, while OXO and Osprey had favorable point-of-sale, and insulated beverageware remained soft.
  • Olivia Tong Cheang, Raymond James: Asked about innovation priorities and discounting in drinkware. Uzzell sees opportunities across the portfolio, prioritizing consumer-centric innovation. Grass discussed normalization in the hydration category and focus on bottles, noting distribution opportunities.
  • Tong Cheang also probed tariff mitigation confidence. Grass said, "We've pulled a lot of the levers, feel good about where we've ended up from a price increase perspective. Only thing that we're being cautious about is how the consumer responds and the elasticity of it."
  • An analyst for Peter Grom, UBS: Asked about volume stabilization assumptions. Schuerman said the outlook assumes "kind of the consistent soft demand trend that we're seeing in the first half" and expects inventory rebalancing and direct import recovery in the second half.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly cautious tone, pressing on portfolio decisions, earnings base, and margin recovery, with questions focused on transitory impacts and volume stabilization.
  • Management's prepared remarks were cautiously optimistic and focused on renewal and execution, while Q&A responses were transparent about ongoing challenges but stressed confidence in mitigation plans. Uzzell's closing remarks maintained a forward-looking, "bullish and excited" tone.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management showed modestly improved confidence, with less defensiveness and a greater focus on strategic initiatives and long-term recovery.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured a CEO transition, bringing renewed focus on culture, innovation, and simplification, in addition to continued emphasis on tariff mitigation and operational effectiveness.
  • Guidance language shifted from providing only quarterly outlooks to offering a full-year forecast, despite ongoing trade disruption.
  • Key metrics such as gross margin, operating margin, and adjusted EPS showed further declines, but management highlighted improved DTC growth and progress on cost controls.
  • Analysts' tone evolved from concern over the depth and duration of tariff impacts to more future-oriented questions about brand revitalization and earnings recovery.
  • Management's tone was more confident, reflecting early progress on strategic priorities and leadership transition, in contrast to the more cautious and defensive stance in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing trade disruption, tariff-related revenue and cost impacts, cautious consumer behavior, and soft demand as major headwinds.
  • Tariff mitigation actions include supplier diversification, inventory management, supplier cost reductions, price increases, and cost management.
  • Management acknowledged that inventory and leverage remain higher than desired and that they may proactively seek additional flexibility from lenders.
  • Analysts repeatedly raised concerns about volume stabilization, elasticity of demand, and the competitive promotional environment, particularly in the hydration category.

Final Takeaway

Helen of Troy's Q2 2026 call marked a leadership handoff and a renewed commitment to innovation, operating effectiveness, and portfolio revitalization. Management acknowledged persistent headwinds from tariffs and soft demand, but pointed to improving operational alignment, robust DTC growth, and targeted pricing actions as key steps toward stabilization. The company set a full-year sales target of $1.74 billion to $1.78 billion and outlined a path toward long-term growth, supported by cost discipline, supply chain diversification, and a sharpened focus on consumer-centric brand building.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Alliance Laundry pops in its IPO open to exceed Whirlpool's market cap
data.description:
Alliance Laundry Holdings (NYSE:ALH) opened for trading on Thursday at $24.50 after the company priced its upsized IPO at $22 per share. The offering included 24.4 million shares sold by the company and 13.2 million shares sold by an existing selling stockholder. Alliance Laundry (NYSE:ALH) will not receive proceeds from the selling stockholder's portion of the sale.  

Shares of Alliance Laundry (ALH) traded as high as $24.60 in early action to value the company above $4.5 billion, just above the market cap of Whirlpool (WHR).

Alliance Laundry Holdings (ALH) is known as the world's leading producer of commercial washing machines and dryers. The Ripon, Wisconsin-based company already has new investors Capital International Investors and Kayne Anderson Rudnick committed to purchasing $200 million worth of shares in the offering.

The company designs and manufactures commercial laundry systems used in healthcare facilities, hotels, laundromats, fire stations, and other institutional settings. In its SEC filing, Alliance Laundry (ALH) said it held an estimated 40% share of the North American commercial laundry market and maintains positions in other international markets.

Alliance Laundry Holdings (ALH) booked $1.6 billion in revenue for the 12 months ended June 30. Net income of $79.0 million was booked for the same period. The company highlighted in its SEC filing that it has achieved a compound annual revenue growth rate of about 9.5% since 2010 and maintains adjusted EBITDA margins around 25%.

Major industry players that compete with Alliance Laundry (ALH) to various degrees include Whirlpool (WHR), Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), LG Electronics, Electrolux AB (OTCPK:ELUXY), Maytag (WHR), Kenmore, and General Electric (GE).
